description_long: This is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. The structure consists of two tall, cylindrical red pillars supporting two horizontal crossbars, with the upper crossbar slightly curved and wider than the lower one. The gate is painted in a deep red color, with a dark brown or black top beam, and features a gold plaque with Japanese characters at the center of the upper crossbar. The base of each pillar is slightly flared, providing stability and a sense of groundedness. The overall design is minimalist yet iconic, symbolizing the transition from the mundane to the sacred. The torii is a prominent cultural and historical landmark in urban parks, temple grounds, and public spaces.
description: A traditional Japanese torii gate with red pillars, a curved dark top beam, and a gold plaque at the center.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gold plaque with Japanese characters and the full width of the curved top beam.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the back of the gate and the absence of the plaque.
description_view_2: Side-rear view showing the structure of the crossbars and the cylindrical pillars.
description_view_3: Rear view with a partial view of the gold plaque and the symmetry of the pillars and crossbars.
